source_jobs.title: Partnerships Manager
source_jobs.description:
Today, 67% of students and 82% of low-income students can’t read at grade level by the end of third grade. That’s when students are supposed to go from learning to read to reading to learn. It’s America’s number one education crisis and we know how to fix it.

Ignite Reading delivers one-to-one virtual tutoring that teaches every student the foundational skills they need to become a confident, fluent reader. We pair students who need extra support with expert reading tutors who provide daily, 15-minute, Science of Reading-based instruction that rapidly closes their decoding gaps. We focus on the foundational reading skills—phonics, phonemic awareness, and sight words—kids need to master so they can decode a sentence. Students in Ignite Reading’s program achieve over 2 weeks of reading skills growth per week of instruction, and notably all students achieve the same outstanding results including students with IEPs, multilingual learners, students of color and those receiving free or reduced-price lunch.

Ignite Reading is a mission-driven public benefit corporation. We believe we can do well for our team members and shareholders while we solve one of the nation’s biggest challenges. We believe we will be more successful with a diverse team and are seeking candidates who have lived experience related to social justice, diversity, equity, and inclusion.

Your Role

As a Partnerships Manager, you are at the heart of our mission to ensure every child has the chance to become a confident reader. You’ll spend your days connecting with small-to-mid-sized K–12 districts, sharing how Ignite Reading’s 1:1 virtual literacy program can change their students' lives.

This is a full-cycle sales journey where your discipline and curiosity shine. You’ll guide districts through thoughtful discovery and clear execution, ensuring our solutions fit their unique needs. While you’ll take the lead on most partnerships, you’re never alone—our senior team is always ready to step in and support you with complex contracts or pricing. If you are a Science of Reading enthusiast who loves building meaningful relationships and wants to help kids access the instruction they deserve, we’d love to meet you.



Key Responsibilities


GENERATE REVENUE & ACQUIRE NEW CLIENTS

 - Prospect, qualify, and close new district opportunities within defined territory parameters

 - Own Discovery → Demo → Proposal → Contract for assigned accounts

 - Meet quarterly and annual quota targets


PIPELINE DEVELOPMENT & PROSPECTING DISCIPLINE

 - Build pipeline through outbound prospecting, events, referrals, and SDR-sourced leads

 - Conduct structured discovery using MEDDPICC methodology

 - Run certified Ignite Reading demos once certified


ACCOUNT SCOPE

 - Focus on small-to-mid-sized districts

 - Navigate multi-stakeholder environments with structured buying processes

 - Escalate enterprise-level or state-wide opportunities to Director/VP


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLLABORATION

 - Partner with Customer Success for smooth handoffs post-close

 - Support pilots as pathways to broader adoption

 - Collaborate with Directors on complex or high-value opportunities when required

Qualifications


K–12 EDTECH SALES FOUNDATION

 - 2–4 years of experience in K–12 education technology sales with consistent quota attainment

 - Experience selling to principals, curriculum directors, literacy coordinators, and school-level leaders

 - Exposure to district procurement processes

 - Demonstrated ability to independently manage small-to-mid-sized district opportunities from prospecting through close


AI-ENABLED SALES DISCIPLINE

 - Active use of AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, or similar) to improve research, outreach personalization, and call preparation

 - Strong CRM discipline and comfort using tools such as HubSpot and conversation intelligence platforms

 - A mindset of optimization — you consistently look for ways to improve efficiency, increase conversion rates, and shorten sales cycles


C

source_jobs.title: Senior Analytics Manager
source_jobs.description:
Today, 67% of students and 82% of low-income students can’t read at grade level by the end of third grade. That’s when students are supposed to go from learning to read to reading to learn. It’s America’s number one education crisis and we know how to fix it.

Ignite Reading delivers one-to-one virtual tutoring that teaches every student the foundational skills they need to become a confident, fluent reader. We pair students who need extra support with expert reading tutors who provide daily, 15-minute, Science of Reading-based instruction that rapidly closes their decoding gaps. We focus on the foundational reading skills—phonics, phonemic awareness, and sight words—kids need to master so they can decode a sentence. Students in Ignite Reading’s program achieve over 2 weeks of reading skills growth per week of instruction, and notably all students achieve the same outstanding results including students with IEPs, multilingual learners, students of color and those receiving free or reduced-price lunch.

Ignite Reading is a mission-driven public benefit corporation. We believe we can do well for our team members and shareholders while we solve one of the nation’s biggest challenges. We believe we will be more successful with a diverse team and are seeking candidates who have lived experience related to social justice, diversity, equity, and inclusion.


YOUR ROLE

Ignite Reading’s mission is to teach every kid to read — and the Product & Program Analytics team sits at the heart of how the organization executes that mission through our Science of Reading-based curriculum. As Senior Manager, you will lead a talented and highly collaborative team to deliver the insights, models, and measurement frameworks that guide our curriculum, product, and intervention strategies.

This is a player-coach role. You will spend roughly 60–70% of your time managing, enabling, and developing your team, and 30–40% doing hands-on analytical work yourself. You will own sprint management, project management, and cross-functional stakeholder relationships with our Product and Academics teams. In addition, you will bring meaningful AI fluency to the team, acting as a leader in our initiatives to accelerate analytical workflows and extend the team’s capacity with agentic workflows and Skills.

This role reports to the VP of Data & Analytics and works in close partnership with the broader Data & Analytics team. It is an exceptional opportunity for a seasoned analytics leader who wants their work to directly shape how millions of students learn to read.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


TEAM LEADERSHIP & DEVELOPMENT

 - Lead, mentor, and develop a team of three: a staff data scientist and two analysts, fostering a collaborative, supportive, and growth-focused environment.

 - Provide technical guidance and hands-on support on high-stakes analytics and data science projects, ensuring the team has the tools, context, and skills needed to do their best work.

 - Actively invest in the team’s AI fluency and utilization, evaluating emerging AI tools and techniques and leveraging Claude Code, Claude Cowork, and related tools to accelerate analytical workflows and extend team capacity.


ANALYTICS & DATA SCIENCE

 - Own and advance program analytics in support of Ignite Reading’s Academics team, providing rigorous measurement of curriculum performance and student success, including assessment against third-party benchmarks such as DIBELS, iReady, and STAR, and conducting analyses to inform what types of interventions to develop and to determine how effective they are.

 - Own and advance product analytics across Ignite Reading’s platform, measuring feature adoption, student engagement, and usage patterns to inform product strategy and prioritization.

 - Help drive the development of the company’s experimentation platform, establishing best practices for experimental design, analysis, and interpretation.

 - Roll up your sleeves alongside the team on selected analytics p

source_jobs.title: VP of Marketing
source_jobs.description:
Today, 67% of students and 82% of low-income students can’t read at grade level by the end of third grade. That’s when students are supposed to go from learning to read to reading to learn. It’s America’s number one education crisis and we know how to fix it.

Ignite Reading delivers one-to-one virtual tutoring that teaches every student the foundational skills they need to become a confident, fluent reader. We pair students who need extra support with expert reading tutors who provide daily, 15-minute, Science of Reading-based instruction that rapidly closes their decoding gaps. We focus on the foundational reading skills—phonics, phonemic awareness, and sight words—kids need to master so they can decode a sentence. Students in Ignite Reading’s program achieve over 2 weeks of reading skills growth per week of instruction, and notably all students achieve the same outstanding results including students with IEPs, multilingual learners, students of color and those receiving free or reduced-price lunch.

Ignite Reading is a mission-driven public benefit corporation. We believe we can do well for our team members and shareholders while we solve one of the nation’s biggest challenges. We believe we will be more successful with a diverse team and are seeking candidates who have lived experience related to social justice, diversity, equity, and inclusion.

Your Role

The literacy crisis is real, well-documented, and solvable. Ignite Reading has the model, the outcomes data, and the momentum—what we need now is a marketing leader who can make sure every district decision-maker in America knows it.

As our Vice President of Marketing, you will be Ignite Reading’s most senior marketing and communications leader and a key member of the Senior Leadership Team. You will own the full marketing funnel from brand awareness and content strategy through demand generation and pipeline contribution with a relentless focus on driving new district adoption and renewal. You will tell our story in a way that reaches and moves district administrators, Directors of Curriculum, and Chief Academic Officers who hold the power to ensure every child has the right to learn to read on time.

The ideal candidate is a true player-coach: a strategic leader with creative instinct who inspires and develops their team, builds strong cross-functional relationships, and brings others along toward a shared vision while also being willing and able to roll up their sleeves and do the work when it matters. At Ignite Reading, we move quickly and operate lean, which means this leader will be as comfortable diving into a HubSpot workflow, analyzing the data, or sharpening the copy on a campaign email as they are presenting a marketing strategy to the CEO, Senior Leadership Team, and Board of Directors. We are looking for someone who leads by example, earns the trust of their team through competence and follow-through, and never considers any task beneath them when the mission demands it.



Key Responsibilities


STRATEGIC MARKETING LEADERSHIP

• Own Ignite Reading's comprehensive annual marketing plan and budget, setting clear goals, KPIs, and resource allocations aligned to district adoption and renewal targets

• Serve as a member of the Senior Leadership Team, contributing to organizational strategy and representing the marketing function at the executive level, ensuring marketing efforts are grounded in data and aligned with organizational capacity

• Partner closely with the VP of Partnerships to align demand generation activities and events with sales pipeline goals

• Build, manage, and mentor a high-performing internal team while overseeing external contractor relationships to ensure that marketing is reaching the right audience with the right message at the right time




DEMAND GENERATION & PIPELINE GROWTH

• In partnership with the Senior Manager of Demand Generation, own and optimize the full demand generation funnel, from top-of-funnel awareness activity (inc

source_jobs.title: Senior Data Scientist
source_jobs.description:
Today, 67% of students and 82% of low-income students can’t read at grade level by the end of third grade. That’s when students are supposed to go from learning to read to reading to learn. It’s America’s number one education crisis and we know how to fix it.

Ignite Reading delivers one-to-one virtual tutoring that teaches every student the foundational skills they need to become a confident, fluent reader. We pair students who need extra support with expert reading tutors who provide daily, 15-minute, Science of Reading-based instruction that rapidly closes their decoding gaps. We focus on the foundational reading skills—phonics, phonemic awareness, and sight words—kids need to master so they can decode a sentence. Students in Ignite Reading’s program achieve over 2 weeks of reading skills growth per week of instruction, and notably all students achieve the same outstanding results including students with IEPs, multilingual learners, students of color and those receiving free or reduced-price lunch.

Ignite Reading is a mission-driven public benefit corporation. We believe we can do well for our team members and shareholders while we solve one of the nation’s biggest challenges. We believe we will be more successful with a diverse team and are seeking candidates who have lived experience related to social justice, diversity, equity, and inclusion.


YOUR ROLE

Ignite Reading's mission is to teach every kid to read — and turning data into decisions is one of the most powerful ways the organization advances that work. In service of that goal, the Senior Data Scientist supports our partner-facing teams (Partnerships/Sales, Marketing, CX) as the Data & Analytics team's primary practitioner of advanced analytics, statistics, predictive modeling, and machine learning. This role is a senior individual contributor who applies DS/ML to generate actionable insights for the business, directly shaping how the organization acquires, retains, and grows partnerships with school districts nationwide.

This person brings meaningful AI fluency to the team, using Claude, Cursor, and related tools to accelerate analytical workflows and build agentic workflows and Skills that extend the team's capacity. This role reports to the Senior Manager of Partner Analytics and works in close partnership with the broader Data & Analytics team. It is an exceptional opportunity for someone who wants to see their work make a real difference that changes lives.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


ADVANCED ANALYTICS & STATISTICAL MODELING

 - Design and deliver end-to-end predictive models (including partner churn prediction, lead scoring, and partner health scores), translating complex business questions into rigorous, production-ready analytical solutions.

 - Develop and maintain attribution, marketing mix models and other marketing analytics frameworks that inform resource allocation and campaign strategy.

 - Apply statistical methods to identify leading indicators, drivers, and patterns across partner data; communicate findings and trade-offs clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders.


DATA SCIENCE & MACHINE LEARNING

 - Own the full ML lifecycle, from problem framing and feature engineering through model training, validation, deployment, and monitoring, ensuring models remain accurate, interpretable, and maintainable over time.

 - Establish and uphold repeatable standards for model development, documentation, and validation that enable consistent, trustworthy DS/ML output across the team.

 - Identify opportunities to apply ML and data science techniques to new business problems and proactively surface recommendations to leadership.


AI ENGINEERING & AGENTIC WORKFLOWS

 - Build and deploy agentic workflows and Claude Skills that automate analytical and modeling tasks, accelerate DS/ML development, and extend the team's capacity.

 - Evaluate and recommend emerging AI tools and techniques relevant to data science and analytics.

 - Act as an AI resou

notes: Small education-focused org with a mix of roles, including tech and non-tech positions.
